name: Morse Code
description: Every letter A-Z and digit 0-9 in International Morse Code, ordered from the shortest codes to the longest. See the code, type the character.

fields.front: Which character is · in Morse code?
fields.extra: · = E. The single dit; Morse gave the most common English letter the shortest code.

fields.front: Which character is − in Morse code?
fields.extra: − = T. The single dah; the second most common letter gets the other one-signal code.

fields.front: Which character is ·− in Morse code?
fields.extra: ·− = A. Dit dah; the exact reverse of N (−·).

fields.front: Which character is ·· in Morse code?
fields.extra: ·· = I. Two dits.

fields.front: Which character is −− in Morse code?
fields.extra: −− = M. Two dahs.

fields.front: Which character is −· in Morse code?
fields.extra: −· = N. Dah dit; the exact reverse of A (·−).

fields.front: Which character is −·· in Morse code?
fields.extra: −·· = D. Dah dit dit; the reverse of U (··−).

fields.front: Which character is −−· in Morse code?
fields.extra: −−· = G. Dah dah dit; the reverse of W (·−−).

fields.front: Which character is −·− in Morse code?
fields.extra: −·− = K. Dah dit dah; sent alone it means over, an invitation to transmit.

fields.front: Which character is −−− in Morse code?
fields.extra: −−− = O. Three dahs; the middle of SOS (··· −−− ···).

fields.front: Which character is ·−· in Morse code?
fields.extra: ·−· = R. Dit dah dit; a lone R means roger, message received.

fields.front: Which character is ··· in Morse code?
fields.extra: ··· = S. Three dits. SOS (··· −−− ···) was chosen as the distress signal because the pattern is unmistakable, not because it abbreviates anything.

fields.front: Which character is ··− in Morse code?
fields.extra: ··− = U. Dit dit dah; the reverse of D (−··).

fields.front: Which character is ·−− in Morse code?
fields.extra: ·−− = W. Dit dah dah; the reverse of G (−−·).

fields.front: Which character is −··· in Morse code?
fields.extra: −··· = B. Dah dit dit dit; the reverse of V (···−).

fields.front: Which character is −·−· in Morse code?
fields.extra: −·−· = C. The rhythm of CO-ca CO-la: DAH di DAH di.

fields.front: Which character is ··−· in Morse code?
fields.extra: ··−· = F. Dit dit dah dit; the reverse of L (·−··).

fields.front: Which character is ···· in Morse code?
fields.extra: ···· = H. Four dits.

fields.front: Which character is ·−−− in Morse code?
fields.extra: ·−−− = J. Dit dah dah dah.

fields.front: Which character is ·−·· in Morse code?
fields.extra: ·−·· = L. Dit dah dit dit; the reverse of F (··−·).

fields.front: Which character is ·−−· in Morse code?
fields.extra: ·−−· = P. Dit dah dah dit; a palindrome, it reads the same forwards and backwards.

fields.front: Which character is −−·− in Morse code?
fields.extra: −−·− = Q. The rhythm of God SAVE the QUEEN: dah dah di dah.

fields.front: Which character is ···− in Morse code?
fields.extra: ···− = V. The opening of Beethoven's Fifth, dit dit dit DAH; V for victory.

fields.front: Which character is −··− in Morse code?
fields.extra: −··− = X. Dah dit dit dah; a palindrome, it reads the same forwards and backwards.

fields.front: Which character is −·−− in Morse code?
fields.extra: −·−− = Y. Dah dit dah dah; the reverse of Q (−−·−).

fields.front: Which character is −−·· in Morse code?
fields.extra: −−·· = Z. Dah dah dit dit; the reverse of M plus I.

fields.front: Which character is ·−−−− in Morse code?
fields.extra: ·−−−− = 1. Digits follow a pattern: one dit, then dahs to fill five signals.

fields.front: Which character is ··−−− in Morse code?
fields.extra: ··−−− = 2. Two dits, then dahs to fill five signals.

fields.front: Which character is ···−− in Morse code?
fields.extra: ···−− = 3. Three dits, two dahs.

fields.front: Which character is ····− in Morse code?
fields.extra: ····− = 4. Four dits, one dah.

fields.front: Which character is ····· in Morse code?
fields.extra: ····· = 5. Five dits.

fields.front: Which character is −···· in Morse code?
fields.extra: −···· = 6. One dah, four dits; from 6 to 0 the dahs come first.

fields.front: Which character is −−··· in Morse code?
fields.extra: −−··· = 7. Two dahs, three dits.

fields.front: Which character is −−−·· in Morse code?
fields.extra: −−−·· = 8. Three dahs, two dits.

fields.front: Which character is −−−−· in Morse code?
fields.extra: −−−−· = 9. Four dahs, one dit.

fields.front: Which character is −−−−− in Morse code?
fields.extra: −−−−− = 0. Five dahs.
